Transcrição Completa: Scaling Math with Jeremy Haynes

[00:10] Yeah, she was on Shark Tank. This is Wendy the Gnome Lady. Okay. You never heard of Wendy the Gnome lady? Okay. Well, here's what her business is. Okay. So, she gets on Shark Tank dressed like a gnome.

[06:05] You know, this is a wake-up call for you. I put this in front of you today because so many of you, okay, you deal with the same shit for so long and I'm so fed up with it. You know, you should be accelerating hundreds of percent per year in company growth, in year-over-year revenue.
[06:23] Yet, some of you tell me like, "Oh, I made $100,000 a month." And then you contract back down to like 60,000 a month and stay there for a fucking year, and then you get back up to 100K a month. Like, I'm going to be proud of you. You know, it's embarrassing.
[06:37] You make people money for a living above and beyond what they pay you. No. Yet the lady selling gnomes is crushing you. Let's do some scaling math, okay? So you can get serious about your life here so we can make some real fucking money, you know? Is that fair?
[06:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Scaling Math Exercise"
NÚMEROS: 2022 annual revenue, 2021 annual revenue, cálculo de gap percentual
VISUAL: Framework de análise financeira, fórmula de cálculo de discrepância, layout de exercício prático
[06:56] Pen and paper out. Let's do some numbers. Okay. What you're going to do first is you're going to write down what you made last year, 2022. Put your annual revenue. Write 2022 and then put your annual revenue that you made, the gross annual revenue.
[07:11] Below that, you're going to write 2021. You're going to put your gross annual revenue for that year. If you weren't in business that year, right up to the recent year. So, you got your 2022 annual revenue. You got your 2021 annual revenue.
[07:26] Okay, it's April right now. A lot of you probably didn't do any kind of yearly goals. If you did, that's great. Are they still top of mind for you? You know, when's the last time you actually held yourself accountable to the statistics of your business and looked at the KPIs you got going on and said, "Am I actually on track to make some real fucking money this year?"
[07:46] Or are you going to make a fucking ass like 13% growth, you know, or like a 20% growth this year? What's it going to take to hit the real number?
[07:54] So, a lot of you guys when you first join into the inner circle, what's the question I ask you?
[07:59] How much you making?
[08:00] Yeah, I ask that. I'm like, "Hey, how much you making right now?" And then what do I ask after that?
[08:05] How much you want to make?
[08:06] There you go. And what did most of you tell me? You tell me a stepping stone goal. You'll be like, "Yeah, dude. I'm making like 40k a month right now. I want to make 100k a month or I want to make like 250 a month." And I'm like, "That's sick. What's the real goal?"
[08:22] And then literally 100% of the time, you then tell me a completely different number that's far bigger than the stepping stones goal that you just articulated to me.
[08:31] So, what you're going to write down next is the real goal of what you actually want to be making per year, and you're going to divide it by 12. Feel free to use your calculator. This is in school. So, your true real annual revenue goal, if you think of it monthly, that's great. Skip ahead and just write down the monthly number of what you truly want to make. Not the stepping stone goal, the real fucking number.
[08:54] Now, I want you to write down after you've got that number what you're currently making per month. Now, you're going to need your calculator for this one. Don't get distracted when you pull out your phone.
[09:05] You're going to take the number that you are currently making. Now, punch it into the calculator. Going to press equals, and you're going to get a decimal percentage. Move the decimal two to the right. That's the percentage of how far off you are right now from that actual goal.
[09:21] I'm gonna say it again. You're going to take the current amount you're making per month right now. You're going to divide it by the real monthly goal that you have, not the stepping stone goal, the real number.
[09:32] So, as an example, you might be making $80,000 a month right now. You're going to divide that by, let's say you want to make a million dollar per month. So, 80,000 divided by a million. And you're going to press equal and we're going to get a percentage. Okay? Technically a decimal. So, you're going to move the decimal two places to the right to make it a percentage.
[09:51] Does that make sense? Does anybody have any questions on the math so far? Okay. Throw out some percentages. How far off are you from the real goal?

[10:02] George, 300% off.
[10:05] Got it. Jordan.

[11:37] Now, we got to ask ourselves, we got to get serious, right? We got to we got to get to the point where that number accelerates so we can crush that number faster. Ask yourself genuinely, and do you actually believe it? Is it possible to make that all this year before December 31st?
[11:55] Is it possible to have a 300% jump? Is it possible to have a 90% jump? Is it possible to have whatever your individual percentage was? Yes or no?
[12:04] No. Don't just say it out loud. It's an honest internal question. And do you legitimately believe it when you say it? Yes or no? And if no, we have to make it believable. And if yes, what the fuck are you doing?
[12:20] If it's yes, why in the world aren't you doing it? You might say to yourself, "Oh, well, I'm on track to do it, Jeremy. That's why I said yes." Are you? Let's ask that question next. If you truly say yes to that question, that you genuinely believe that you're on track to hit that from a statistical perspective so far this year, are you?
[12:40] Yes or no?
[12:42] What's the problem? It's not one static answer across the room. It's a dynamic answer. You know what the fuck problem is. Could be a lot of shit. It could be you're lazy. You still haven't actualized on that discipline that's inside of you to actually do what the fuck you need to do every day.
[13:00] You know, you find yourself doing shit with your time. I'm not telling you you need to be working 16 hours a day. I'm telling you you need to do the right things in a day that actually are going to push that business to that percentage that you're missing out on right now to hit the real goal.
[13:17] How long are you currently anticipating it's going to take to make up that gap? What's the answer to yourself? Do you say, "Oh, well, I planned on doing it this year." Or or did you not have a number at all? How long's it going to take to hit the number? Do you have an answer? Or is it just like a theoretical I'd be nice if I could hit it, you know?
[13:37] I'd be able to buy some nice shit. Pretty comfortable where I'm at, though. You know, I'm happy. Do you have that inside of you?
[13:45] I come before you today as somebody who was just like that. Who used to simply tell myself, "Oh, you know, I want to make a million a month. There's a lot of cool things I could do with a million a month. I'm sure." But then I got serious about the number.
[14:00] I started changing the offer structure we had. I started pushing my deals farther. I started asking for higher percentages on deals we did. The types of clients I was unwilling to work with got stiff armed more often. The types of clients that were necessary and I had to have to hit a million a month became the only targets.
[14:20] Anybody that fell short of our standard, it's not even an option to work with them. The things I need to do in a day, I fucking do. You know why? Cuz I'm actually serious about the number.
[14:31] I ask you a simple question. Cuz for me, I thought I was serious about it. But then when I actually changed my behavior, I saw a rapid advancement and then exceeded the number.
[14:42] What is holding you back from the number? Write it down. Put it right below that percentage gap. Put the real reasons. And I'm sure there's more than one. Bullet point them out. What is holding you back? Be honest about it. Don't lie about it. You're only bullshitting yourself another however long you're going to tolerate that version of you.
[15:05] Think about it. It's just another monster on the other side of that fence that you've got to go and tackle. Think about it. It does it really lower your quality of life to just do a different set of actions in a day with the same time you're currently working?
[15:20] I'm not asking you to work harder. I've worked less as I've made more money. Okay? Yeah, I do a lot of shit in a day still, but the amount of time I've worked has not exceeded the same threshold I normally work in total hours a day.
[15:35] I'm not asking you to work more. I'm asking you, what's that thing that's holding you back? Throw a few of them out. And I ask you this before you answer. Put a little asterisk next to the one that you really know is the fucking main thing, okay?
[15:50] Cuz for me, there was a there was a list of things, you know? There was a bullet point list for sure, but there was one main fucking thing that I really identified as, you know, definitely this. If I just did this and I did all these other auxiliary things, I'd push that fucking needle hard. Okay.

[17:48] You know, and what like what what's going to what's going to kick the fire under your ass? Yeah, I've got a little bit of money coming in. Yeah, I'm in the top whatever 1 percentile of planet Earth. Who gives a fuck?
[18:42] You know, you're going to have to work until you're like 70 at your current pace cuz you don't put shit aside. You don't have nearly enough that you've set aside to actually stop working for active income to keep up your current lifestyle that you built for yourself given your expenses.
[18:58] You know, you're going to go back to shopping at fucking groceries at Walmart if you fucking settled and retired with the amount that you've invested at this stage in your life. You know, you're going to live in a small rinky dink fucking apartment with mold.
[19:13] If you keep fucking around, you're gonna be one of those guys who has to settle with a Camry or a Corolla and brags about it on Twitter, you know? Oh, I like living with a Corolla in my fucking little ass house in Ohio. You know, you know what it really is? [20:13] What's going to make it change? What's going to make you think life is incremental progress? That's your issue. You think life's like a game of stairs. You know, you act like tomorrow you can't wake up a whole different fucking monster.
[20:28] You act like you have to incrementally improve, you know, and read another fucking book or like get another ass whooping from Jeremy on a one-on-one call to finally wake up. You can do it's a choice. It's a choice that you're doing all this all this whack shit that's holding you back.

[23:37] Ryan sat in this very house. You watched me if you were in this inner circle in Q4. I handed him a trophy for making $5 million a month. Okay. He sat in this room upstairs in this house. [24:02] Nothing.

[24:20] 1.1.

[24:46] Pain.
[24:47] Pain. Were you in pain?

[25:01] Most like wake up just want to throw up every day, you know?
[25:05] Yeah. How was it for you? Did you want to throw up? Were you physically ill?
[25:10] Yeah. I didn't have any money to save up to.
[25:13] Yeah. Fucking right?
[25:15] Yeah.
[25:16] You see you right now sitting here, what was the reason you wrote down the real reason that the change is going to occur? You think it's going to be pleasure, right? You think it's going to be like, I just want to make a million dollars a month, you know? I want to hit my fucking goal. I want to buy Ferraris and shit.
[25:34] It's like, that's not going to do it. You know what's going to do it? It's going to be getting fucking disgusted with how small you are playing this game right now. How intolerable are you gonna be of yourself?
[25:47] When are you gonna instead of doing all this, oh dude, I'm doing great. You know, I'm making a couple hundred grand a month. It's like, CONGRATFUCKULATIONS. YOU'RE STILL POOR AND YOU KNOW IT. YOU KNOW YOU DON'T HAVE even remotely close to enough.
[26:03] You know that you're 90 plus hundreds of percent off some of you from hitting the real fucking number. And yet you don't recognize the pain that you should be experiencing. Is it really going to take going to zero dollars for you? For you to finally fucking kick it into gear?
[26:22] Is that really what it's going to take to see performance from you? Going to literally zero? Cuz if it is, just shut your business down so you actually do something with your fucking life.
[26:33] You know, if you're going to keep saying, "Oh, I'm comfortable enough." You know, and you're just going to keep riding this wave of habits and patterns that you've established and you're going to make no change otherwise. Shut your fucking business down, experience zero dollars, and kick it into gear so you can burn it down and start it up the way it needs to be running again.
[26:54] If that's the kind of person that you are, where you literally only perform when your back's against the wall, how does that sound? Does that sound like a healthy place to operate from? To have to literally go to zero to finally perform? It clearly works, right? We can't we can't deny that, right?
[27:13] Is that how you want to be? You want to take it to zero to finally be a top earner? It shouldn't be like that, right? We as a human being should be capable of just simply taking our current position, looking at it for what it truly is, which is not even close to where you actually want to get to.
[27:32] You should be able to feel it, you know, like that level of like, man, this is all I'm fucking capable of. It's like you guys are benching the bar right now and you're proud of yourself. I'm not here to beat you down. I'm here to wake you up to the mediocrity that you've been living in day after day after day after day after day until some shit comes and just kicks your life out from underneath you and you finally fucking wake up.
[27:58] What a fucking position to have to operate from, but you'll get out of it because you have to, just like those guys did. Why not instead be capable of simply doing the same thing without having to take it down that far? Do you think that's possible of yourself?
[28:16] Ask yourself genuinely, Jason, do you think you could have done it if if you didn't get life taken out from underneath you? You think you would you think you would have genuinely been capable of doing it? Or you think that's what you had to go through to get to where you are now?
[28:32] Yeah, I think I will never afford myself to do it.
[28:36] Nothing else would have woken you up. Huh?
[28:39] I thought I thought I was rich already when I
[28:42] Exactly what I'm saying. You see, and that that mental shift, what did it take? It took the mental shift, right? Because you judge yourself on external circumstances. It took the fact that you had 30K coming in to feel that way, didn't it?
[28:57] So you judged yourself on external circumstances that were happening to you to then internally make a shift that then resulted in a significantly different external world. Why don't you have the ability to internally make a shift without external shit having to signal to you when to shift internally?
[29:17] What a power that would be. No, just to say, "Oh, I can choose. I can fucking feel that exact same thing without having to go through the circumstances of it. Do you recognize the power of your brain? Are you waking up to what I'm saying yet?
[29:33] It's either going to be a continuous cycle of comfort because you're addicted to judging yourself based on your external circumstances. John, how many plates do you throw on that fucking bench or squat nowadays? The 45 plates when you bench.
[29:48] Jesus Christ. Five 45 pound plates. when this guy benches. Did you Did you Was that an external thing that told you that you'd be capable of doing that or did you internally push yourself to that point to be able to bench that much?
[30:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Internal vs External Motivation Framework"
NÚMEROS: 5 placas de 45 libras cada lado no supino
VISUAL: Comparação entre motivação interna (auto-determinada) vs externa (circunstancial), analogia de força física vs mental
[30:07] He fucking pushed himself. Do you know any person besides JC sitting here who benches five fucking plates on both sides besides literal power lifters? I don't even think powerlifters do that. Internally pushed himself recognized he was capable of more.
[30:24] That doesn't mean he can just go from a 45 pound plate to five 45 pound plates. That's a physical strength issue. Guess what is not a physical strength issue? Money. Money is a fucking number on a screen nowadays.
[30:37] You guys aren't clearing cash and using Brinks to transfer money between your digital business to a fucking bank. It's all digital shit. You make up the number you charge. You make it up. Why not make up a better number?
[30:52] You wake up and you get to choose what you do in a day. Who tells you you have to do what you do in a day right now? You have some fucking boss that's like, "Hey, dude. This is what I demand of you today." Who chooses? Say it.
[31:07] Yeah, that's fucking right. And you're choosing to do shit. Are you going to wait for the external circumstance to finally wake you up? Is that really what it's going to take? Your life to crumple before you finally fucking snap and say, "I'm finally going to do what I should have been doing this whole time."
[31:27] It's a game of actualizing on potential after you get to the point of comfort. It's a It's a game. It's a game to push it further. It's a game to make more. It's just a leaderboard. You don't have to make more, guys. You don't have to.
[31:43] You could all fucking retire right now in Kentucky and live a fucking life in a little fucking one-story house that's two bedrooms with a Corolla. You got that set up for yourself. Congratulations. You got a foundation.
[31:56] But is that how you want it to be? Is that how any of you actually want to fucking live? Eating Walmart ribeye? I doubt it. I fucking doubt it. And look, making more money is sick. It's great. You have more options. You have more fun, but you get it at a certain point.
[32:14] It's just, yeah, you get a bigger, you get a nicer car, you know, you get a bigger boat, get a bigger fucking house, you know, you get a bigger yard, you get the purebred dog instead of the fucking shelter dog, you know, like little things, you know?
[32:27] You get to eat the best 60-day dry-aged rum infused fucking thick ass ribeyes from the best cows on planet Earth instead of the Walmart ribeyes, right? But it's like, you know, is that it for you? Is that really all you're going to do as a fucking human being on Earth is selfishly be able to eat food, live in an apartment, and pay for a few cars?
[32:50] You're playing the game like a fucking loser, and you know it. Yet, you're waiting until life gets kicked out from underneath you to finally push it. What a weird mental frame. No.
[33:03] What's it going to be? What's it going to be? That's all I ask. What's it going to fucking be? Is tomorrow finally the day? Are you finally going to leave here and actually make more fucking money? A serious amount of money?
[33:17] Are you finally going to make up that percentage that you're that you're at a loss for right now? Or are you just going to keep making these weak excuses about how things are?
[33:28] You've done everything to yourself. You've done it all. You've put yourself in these circumstances. You're making up the excuses that you think you're experiencing. Just make the change. Just make the change to finally do it. Easy enough.
[33:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Truth About Your Excuses"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Framework de auto-responsabilidade, todas as circunstâncias são escolhas, conceito de história fabricada
[33:46] Look at your reasons again. Look how full of shit you are. You think it's those random things. This is all you need to write down. It's all made up. It's all a choice. All of it. All of the circumstances you view right now. All of the situations you think you're in, the whole fucking story you've told yourself about why it's the way it is, why you're charging what you charge, all of it is completely fabricated by you.
[34:14] Why not rewrite the story? Fair enough.
[34:18] Okay. You have questions on this? Anything?
[34:23] Yeah. Better than AI tools, huh? Yeah, I just, you know, I thought it'd be I thought it'd be more appropriate to talk about this. Okay. Well, yeah, that's it. We're going to break for lunch.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Wendy Gnome Lady: $686K em 18 meses → $13M desde 2020
Banana Art Basel: $120,000
Fart Jar Girl: $280,000
Belle Delphine: $2M/mês vendendo água de banho
Cash Me Outside Girl: $52M lifetime (~$26M/ano, idade 19)
Bombas Socks: Vendido por $100M, $8.3M anuais
Jason (FAJA): $30K/mês → $0 → $980K/mês em 6 meses → Vendeu por $8M em 2 anos
Ryan: $0 (Janeiro) → $1.1M (Abril) → $5.5M/mês em menos de 1 ano
Maior earner do grupo: $750K/mês
Meta do grupo: Maioria 90-300% abaixo do objetivo real
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Scaling Math Framework:
Anotar receita 2022 e 2021
Definir meta real mensal (não stepping stone)
Calcular gap percentual: (Atual ÷ Meta) × 100
Identificar razões reais (não superficiais)
Colocar asterisco na razão principal
Mudanças de Comportamento para Crescimento:
Mudar estrutura de oferta
Aumentar percentuais em deals
Filtrar tipos de clientes rigorosamente
Fazer apenas ações que movem a agulha
Trabalhar menos horas, mais efetivamente
Motivação por Dor vs Prazer:
Dor (escala 10/10) gera ação massiva
Conforto gera estagnação
Não esperar chegar a $0 para agir
Criar urgência interna sem necessidade de crise externa
ESTRUTURA:
Inner Circle Group (grupo de alto desempenho)
Accountability baseada em KPIs e estatísticas
Meta não é incremental, é transformacional
Mindset: "Want the deal but don't need the deal"
Foco: Fazer dinheiro para clientes acima do que investem
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Benchmark de "sucesso medíocre": $100K-250K/mês
Benchmark de alto desempenho: $750K+/mês
Timeline de transformação comprovada: 2-6 meses para resultados massivos
ROI para clientes: Múltiplos milhões vs investimento
Meta de aposentadoria: Capital suficiente para renda passiva aos 35-40 anos
LIÇÕES PRINCIPAIS:
Produtos "ridículos" (gnomos, água de banho, peidos) superam profissionais de marketing
Gap entre receita atual e meta real é 90-300% para maioria
Mudança real vem de dor extrema ou escolha consciente interna
Todas as circunstâncias são escolhas, não destino
Conforto é o maior inimigo do crescimento exponencial
Dinheiro é digital - você escolhe o número que cobra
Transformação pode acontecer em meses, não anos
DURAÇÃO: ~34 minutos FORMATO: Coaching presencial confrontacional com exercícios práticos de scaling math e estudos de caso reais
